prose: Warfarin is correctly returned as negative under an adversarial-negative tier, and the reasoning intentionally withholds any Parkinson's flag despite measurable bioactivity. The decisive basis is curated mechanism: there is no CTD curated DirectEvidence linking warfarin to Parkinson's and it is not a registered neuro-AOP stressor [E1]. The 6 mechanistic ToxCast assay hits — notably 0 mitochondrial — are exactly the kind of imposter activity signal the engine treats as corroboration only, never as diagnostic, so bioactivity alone does not upgrade the call [E2]. Two orthogonal strands reinforce the negative: FAERS shows no parkinsonism signal across 126,794 reports (ROR 0.5, not elevated) [E3], and structure-based partitioning predicts low, BBB-negative brain exposure, a pharmacokinetic reason it is implausible as a CNS driver [E4]. On the evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis, warfarin lacks any curated causal footing; no causation is claimed, and the negative call stands.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 6 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 0 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 126794 FAERS reports (ROR 0.5, not elevated).
Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
